name: tapvc
description: term 3.5 kg neonate with (unobstructed, supracardiac) total anomalous pulmonary venous connection: all pulmonary venous return drains to the superior vena cava and mixes with systemic venous return in the right atrium, and the left heart is filled only by an obligate right-to-left shunt across the foramen ovale; produces mild cyanosis from complete mixing and is not duct-dependent (a foramen-ovale-dependent lesion)

Drugs.name: Drugs
Drugs.description: pharmacology (PK/PD) controller — Milestone 1: adrenaline → heart rate

LA.name: LA
LA.description: timevarying elastance model of the left atrium

RAIVCI.name: RAIVCI
RAIVCI.description: timevarying elastance model of the right atrium

RASVC.description: timevarying elastance model of the right atrium

RV.description: timevarying elastance model of the right ventricle

Hormones.description: RAAS / ADH neuro-hormonal volume & osmolality control

EF_MV.description: ans effector pathway on minute volume

COR_RASVC.description: resistor model of the connection between the coronaries and the right atrium

HeartFunction.name: HeartFunction
HeartFunction.description: load-induced ventricular contractility compromise (afterload mismatch + dilation, acute + remodeling)

GASEX_LL.description: gas exchanger model of the blood-gas connection of the left lung

GASEX_RL.name: GASEX_RL
GASEX_RL.description: gas exchanger model of the blood-gas connection of the right lung

model_definition.name: tapvc
model_definition.description: term 3.5 kg neonate with (unobstructed, supracardiac) total anomalous pulmonary venous connection: all pulmonary venous return drains to the superior vena cava and mixes with systemic venous return in the right atrium, and the left heart is filled only by an obligate right-to-left shunt across the foramen ovale; produces mild cyanosis from complete mixing and is not duct-dependent (a foramen-ovale-dependent lesion)
